originalAlarm.description: The call to `prepend_new_eth_table` may trigger the following issue: `ch` could be null (from the call to `prepend_new_eth_table()` on line 40) and is dereferenced in the call to `prepend_new_eth_table()`.

reasoning: Line 40: prepend_new_eth_table() called from main(). Inside that function, `ch = malloc(sizeof(*ch))` at L29 has no NULL check, then `ch->ifname = ifname` at L30 dereferences ch — null dereference if malloc returns NULL. prepend_new_eth_table() is outside any #ifdef, so the bug occurs in all configs. No '// ERROR' at L29/L30 → new bug not in VBDB. PC requires CLEAN_UP=T but the null dereference exists regardless of CLEAN_UP. Over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
